note: Aggregate counts only. No row-level or free-text data is included.

cohort.text: Which founder community are you part of?

questions.text: What is your role?
What is your age?
What is your gender?
What is your race/ethnicity?
What industry is your company in?
How many people are on your team? (employees + full-time contractors, including yourself)
How much total funding has your company raised?
What is your approximate annual run rate?
What year was your company founded?
Overall, how satisfied are you with life as a whole these days?
In general, how happy or unhappy do you usually feel?
Overall, to what extent do you feel the things you do in your life are worthwhile?
I understand my purpose in life.
My relationships are as satisfying as I would want them to be.
In general, how would you rate your physical health?
How would you rate your overall mental health?
Most of what I do as a founder feels like 'I have to' rather than 'I want to.'
I feel alone carrying the weight of this company.
I keep getting in my own way.
I struggle to evolve from being a technical/functional contributor to being a leader.
I am trapped in operational details instead of being strategic.
I feel like a fraud or pretend leader.
I struggle to hold my team accountable.
I avoid hard calls and difficult conversations I know I need to have.
Our team is moving too slowly given what we need to accomplish.
There is too much friction between me and my cofounder(s).
I am dealing with conflict with my board or investors.
I am worried we will run out of money.
I am worried we won't be able to raise our next round.
I am worried we may need to pivot the company.
We are not growing fast enough.
I am anxious about competition.
What is your primary cofounder's gender?
What is your primary cofounder's role?
My cofounder and I are aligned on where we want to take the company.
My cofounder and I share the same standards for quality and excellence.
I trust my cofounder to do what they say they'll do.
I can be honest with my cofounder about my doubts, fears, and mistakes.
When my cofounder and I disagree, we work through it rather than avoid it.
I can raise difficult topics with my cofounder without it damaging the relationship.
My cofounder and I have clear, agreed-upon roles and decision rights.
The division of labor between my cofounder and me feels fair.
Overall, how would you rate the health of your cofounder relationship today?
I am ambitious.
I strive for success.
I have challenging goals.
I am pursuing ambitious goals in more than one domain of my life (e.g., work, creative work, physical, relational, spiritual).
My identity is primarily defined by what I do professionally.
How important to you is the following: Helping others improve their lives.
How important to you is the following: Knowing and accepting who I really am.
How important to you is the following: Being financially successful.
How important to you is the following: Being admired and recognized by many people.
I am working on my company because other people (investors, peers, family) expect me to, and there would be real consequences if I quit.
I am working on my company because of the rewards it can bring me — money, status, recognition.
I am working on my company because I would feel guilty or like a failure if I quit.
I am working on my company because I genuinely value what we're building, regardless of how it turns out.
I am working on my company because it's an expression of who I am at my core — coherent with my other values and the rest of my life.
I am working on my company because I find the work itself genuinely enjoyable.
I am working on my company because — honestly, I'm not sure why anymore.
Little interest or pleasure in doing things
Feeling down, depressed, or hopeless
Trouble falling or staying asleep, or sleeping too much
Feeling tired or having little energy
Poor appetite or overeating
Feeling bad about yourself — or that you are a failure or have let yourself or your family down
Trouble concentrating on things, such as reading the newspaper or watching television
Moving or speaking so slowly that other people could have noticed? Or the opposite — being so fidgety or restless that you have been moving around a lot more than usual
Thoughts that you would be better off dead or of hurting yourself in some way
Feeling nervous, anxious, or on edge
Not being able to stop or control worrying
Worrying too much about different things
Trouble relaxing
Being so restless that it is hard to sit still
Becoming easily annoyed or irritable
Feeling afraid as if something awful might happen
I feel emotionally drained from my work.
I feel used up at the end of a workday.
I feel tired when I get up in the morning and have to face another day on the job.
I have become less interested in my work since I started this job.
I have become less enthusiastic about my work.
I have become more cynical about whether my work contributes anything.
In my opinion, I am good at my job.
I feel exhilarated when I accomplish something at work.
I have accomplished many worthwhile things in this job.

composites.label: Professional Efficacy
composites.description: Mean of 3 items (0–6, higher = more efficacy)

questions.text: How often do you have trouble wrapping up the final details of a project, once the challenging parts have been done?
How often do you have difficulty getting things in order when you have to do a task that requires organization?
How often do you have problems remembering appointments or obligations?
When you have a task that requires a lot of thought, how often do you avoid or delay getting started?
How often do you fidget or squirm with your hands or feet when you have to sit down for a long time?
How often do you feel overly active and compelled to do things, like you were driven by a motor?
I often notice small sounds when others do not.
I usually concentrate more on the whole picture, rather than small details.
I find it easy to do more than one thing at once.
If there is an interruption, I can switch back to what I was doing very quickly.
I find it easy to 'read between the lines' when someone is talking to me.
I know how to tell if someone listening to me is getting bored.
When I'm reading a story, I find it difficult to work out the characters' intentions.
I like to collect information about categories of things (e.g., types of car, types of bird, types of train, types of plant).
I find it easy to work out what someone is thinking or feeling just by looking at their face.
I find it difficult to work out people's intentions.
Have you ever been formally diagnosed with ADHD?
Have you ever been formally diagnosed with autism or Asperger's?
Have you ever been formally diagnosed with any other neurodivergent condition (e.g., dyslexia, dyscalculia, Tourette's)?
I have used deceit or lied to get my way.
I have used flattery to get my way.
I tend to manipulate others to get my way.
I tend to exploit others towards my own end.
I tend to lack remorse.
I tend to be unconcerned with the morality of my actions.
I tend to be callous or insensitive.
I tend to be cynical.
I tend to want others to admire me.
I tend to want others to pay attention to me.
I tend to seek prestige or status.
I tend to expect special favors from others.
How many people outside your company could you confide in about work struggles?
How many people outside your company could you confide in about personal struggles?
In the past month, how many times did you confide in someone outside your company about work struggles?
In the past month, how many times did you confide in someone outside your company about personal struggles?
Have you ever worked with a therapist or counselor?
Are you currently in therapy?
How long have you been with your current therapist?
Have you ever worked with an executive, leadership, or other professional coach?
Are you currently working with a coach?
What kind of coach?
In the last 12 months, have you considered seeking mental health support but didn't?
In the last 12 months, have you taken a mental health leave or extended break from work?
How would you rate the overall impact of therapy on your life?
How would you rate the overall impact of coaching on your life?
What got in the way? (select all that apply)

options.label: Didn't think my issues were serious enough
Couldn't find someone who understood founders
Concerned about stigma or what investors/employees would think
Previous bad experience with mental health care

questions.text: Are you currently taking any of the following? Select all that apply. Responses are anonymous.

options.label: SSRI or SNRI antidepressant (e.g., Lexapro, Zoloft, Prozac, Wellbutrin, Effexor)
Stimulant for ADHD (e.g., Adderall, Vyvanse, Concerta, Ritalin)
Prescription sleep medication (e.g., Ambien, Lunesta, trazodone)
Beta blocker for performance or anxiety (e.g., propranolol)
Mood stabilizer or antipsychotic (e.g., lithium, lamotrigine, quetiapine)

questions.text: Alcohol
Cannabis / marijuana
Nicotine (cigarettes, vapes, pouches)
Stimulants obtained without a prescription (e.g., Adderall, cocaine)
MDMA
Psilocybin ("magic mushrooms")
Ayahuasca or DMT
LSD or other psychedelics
Ketamine (recreational, not prescribed)
